tools.name: search_memories
tools.description: Searches semantically across stored memories using Mem0. Use this to retrieve relevant context about user preferences, patterns, and past interactions before responding. This is for retrieval only - use add_memory to store new memories, and list_memories to see all memories.

query.description: Search query text to find relevant memories

limit.description: Maximum number of memories to return

user.description: User ID to search memories for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: add_memory
tools.description: Adds new memories to Mem0 storage. Use this when user explicitly asks to remember something or to store conversation context for future retrieval. Supports two forms: simple text memory or conversation messages (auto-extracts memories via LLM). For searching stored memories, use search_memories instead.

text.description: Simple text memory to store (use instead of messages)

messages.description: Array of conversation messages to extract memories from (use instead of text)

user.description: User ID to store memory for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: list_memories
tools.description: Lists all stored memories for a user with IDs and creation dates. Use this to see what memories are currently stored. For searching specific memories, use search_memories instead.

user.description: User ID to list memories for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: delete_memory
tools.description: Deletes memories from Mem0 storage. Use this to remove specific memories or all memories for a user. Supports two modes: delete a specific memory by ID, or delete all memories for a user.

memory_id.description: ID of specific memory to delete (use instead of all flag)

all.description: Delete all memories for the user (use instead of memory_id)

user.description: User ID to delete memories for (required when using all flag)

messages.content:
Great news — there **are** existing preferences and patterns stored. Here's what's currently tracked about your road technology research:

### Key Preferences & Patterns

| Category | Details |
|----------|---------|
| **Pavement Materials** | Preference for **asphalt over concrete** for highways (lower cost, easier maintenance) |
| **Smart Technologies** | Interest in **embedded sensors**, **solar pavements**, **EV charging integrated into roadways** |
| **Sustainability** | Focus on **green roads**, **permeable pavement systems** |
| **Construction** | Preference for **modular techniques** for faster deployment |
| **Planning Approach** | **Data-driven decisions**, interest in **ITS (Intelligent Transportation Systems)** |
| **Research Areas** | Transportation planning, road network optimization, predictive maintenance algorithms |
| **Investment Focus** | Preference for **public transportation infrastructure** over private road projects |

### What's Missing?
No records of specific projects, timelines, or current research initiatives yet — just preferences and interest areas.

Would you like me to:
1. **Store new records** about current projects or initiatives?
2. **Add more detail** to any of these existing preference areas?
3. **List all memories** to see the full picture?

Yes, I'd like to store some information about our current initiatives. We're currently exploring three main road technology projects: a pilot program for inductive charging lanes on a stretch of Highway 7 to support electric vehicles, a partnership with the engineering university on self-healing asphalt using encapsulated rejuvenators, and we're also evaluating smart traffic signal systems that use AI to optimize flow patterns. Could you help me record these as new memories so we can track our progress on each one?

This is a pilot program to evaluate feasibility of on-road wireless charging."}, {"role": "user", "content": "Current Initiative 2: Self-Healing Asphalt Research - Partnership with the engineering university to develop self-healing asphalt using encapsulated rejuvenators. The encapsulated agents release when cracks form, restoring pavement integrity."}, {"role": "user", "content": "Current Initiative 3: AI Smart Traffic Signals - Evaluating intelligent traffic signal systems that use AI to analyze traffic patterns and optimize signal timing in real-time for improved flow and reduced congestion."}]}
